pattern: Binary search on the answer: for a candidate n, compute the accumulated price (sum of prices of 1..n) using bit-pattern counting, then find the largest n where this sum <= k.
hints: The price of num counts set bits at positions x, 2x, 3x, ... (1-indexed from LSB). The accumulated price of n is the sum of prices over all numbers 1..n.
For a fixed bit position p (p = x, 2x, ...), the contribution to the accumulated price of n can be computed analytically: every 2^p numbers form a cycle where exactly 2^(p-1) have bit p set.
Binary search on n in [1, 10^15]: compute accumulated_price(n) in O(log(n)/x * log(n)) time and check if it's <= k.

edge_inputs.description: k=1, x=1 — first number with set bit at position 1 is 1
k=1, x=2 — first number with set bit at position 2 is 2
k exactly reaches a boundary — accumulated price equals k exactly

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 10**12)
    x = rng.randint(1, 8)
    return [k, x]
